perturbation.original_text: These stock options will remain in place until certificates are issued once volumes are met as long as the CELEBRITY maintains the distributorship by paying the annual dues of $15.00.
perturbation.changed_text: These stock options will remain in place until certificates are issued once volumes are met, regardless of whether the CELEBRITY maintains the distributorship by paying the annual dues of $15.00. The stock options will be automatically exercised upon meeting the sales volume requirements, regardless of distributorship status.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction regarding the requirements for retaining the stock options. The original clause states that the options remain in place as long as the CELEBRITY maintains the distributorship by paying annual dues. The altered clause stipulates that the options remain regardless of distributorship status and provides for automatic exercise. This directly conflicts with the original requirement to maintain the distributorship, creating ambiguity about what is required for the stock options to vest.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CELEBRITY will receive $6,000 per month for entire duration of the agreement. The COMPANY will give stock to the CELEBRITY which will be restricted for one year from issuance and will be as follows: Monthly Sales Volume Stock Options -------------------- ------------- $ 300,000 300,000 shares 600,000 600,000 shares 1,000,000 1,000,000 shares These stock options will remain in place until certificates are issued once volumes are met, regardless of whether the CELEBRITY maintains the distributorship by paying the annual dues of $15.00. The stock options will be automatically exercised upon meeting the sales volume requirements, regardless of distributorship status.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original clause regarding the conditions for retaining stock options. The original text states that the options remain in place as long as the CELEBRITY maintains the distributorship by paying annual dues, while the changed text stipulates that the options remain regardless of distributorship status. This creates a clear contradiction and ambiguity about what is required for the stock options to vest, directly impacting the enforceability and understanding of the agreement. The changed text and contradicted text are present in the same location.

perturbation.original_text: TERMS FOR THE CELEBRITY Work with Jack Zufelt to develop a business strategy that can be implemented within 30 days of the signing of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: TERMS FOR THE CELEBRITY Work with Jack Zufelt to develop a business strategy. The implementation of this strategy is subject to a separate agreement and timeline determined at a later date.
perturbation.explanation: The change contradicts the initial agreement to implement the business strategy within 30 days. The modified text states that implementation is subject to a separate agreement and timeline. This directly introduces uncertainty as to when the implementation will actually occur, creating an in-text contradiction by negating the initially agreed-upon 30-day timeframe. It now implies that the implementation timeline can be changed or delayed through another agreement, effectively rendering the original 30-day period non-binding.
perturbation.contradicted_text: TERMS FOR THE CELEBRITY Work with Jack Zufelt to develop a business strategy. The implementation of this strategy is subject to a separate agreement and timeline determined at a later date. (i) Identify marketing platform to recruit new distributors (ii) Develop a recruiting packet for new recruits that are duplicable with most tools coming from the corporate website and / or inventory. (iii) CELEBRITY will be available for phone conferences and meeting participating at any level deemed necessary by the COMPANY. (iv) CELEBRITY will be available for conference calls not to exceed five per month and at the discretion of his schedule. (v) The terms of this agreement will remain confidential between CELEBRITY and the COMPANY unless written permission is granted for release by both parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulated implementation within 30 days. The changed text removes this specific timeline and subjects implementation to a separate agreement, creating an ambiguity and negating the original commitment. This directly contradicts any expectation within the rest of the document that relies on the 30-day implementation timeframe as initially stated. The modified text states: 'The implementation of this strategy is subject to a separate agreement and timeline determined at a later date.' This creates an in-text contradiction because it replaces a specific commitment (30 days) with an indefinite future agreement, making the original clause unenforceable.
